text: Handling deadlines is an essential skill in both work and study. Good time management helps you plan your tasks and avoid last-minute stress. Prioritization is also important: decide which tasks are most urgent or important, and do these first. Sometimes, you may have too much work and need to practice scope management. This means saying no to extra tasks or breaking a big project into smaller parts. Finally, communication is key. If you think you cannot meet a deadline, tell your boss or teacher early. They may give you more time or help you adjust your workload. With these strategies, you can meet deadlines more calmly and confidently.
